title: Enforce 48h separation for rev-3 single-operator reviews, prevent violations at runtime
context: Single-operator experiment protocol (expreg_9c9617cd13, revision ≥3) requires 48 hours between case-bank lock and first review start. Previously this was auditable post-hoc; violations were visible but not prevented.
decision: Move enforcement from auditable constraint to hard runtime check. nextExperimentReview() now throws on violation for rev-3+ runs instead of allowing the operation and logging later. Resumed sessions exempt. Older revisions unaffected.
consequences: Violations impossible rather than post-hoc visible — fail fast during review request
Error message names exact remaining wait time, guiding operator to correct retry
Backwards compatible: revision 1/2 runs bypass check entirely
Single-operator protocol now has teeth: governance becomes technical enforcement
alternatives_rejected: Keep audit-only model (weak: lets bad state through, requires downstream cleanup)
Make enforcement configurable per run (weak: defeats purpose of single-operator guardrail)
Apply to all revisions uniformly (breaks backwards compat)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep audit-only model (weak: lets bad state through, requires downstream cleanup)
Make enforcement configurable per run (weak: defeats purpose of single-operator guardrail)
Apply to all revisions uniformly (breaks backwards compat)
